page: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The underlying processor is an Intel Xeon 8488C running at 3.2 GHz, equipped with a 105 MB L3 cache. Memory configuration consists of 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, yielding a vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 1:8. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance in several areas. The instance achieves top-25% results in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, Passmark CPU scores, and static web serving throughput. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also in the top 25%, though large-block read and write bandwidth tests fall into the middle 50%. The CPU features advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which contribute to strong performance in LLM inference tasks for small and medium models, as well as software compilation and cryptographic operations.

The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads despite a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density and DDR5 speed provide a balanced resource profile for memory-intensive applications without the overhead of GPU hardware. This server is configured for workloads such as database operations, memory-cached applications, enterprise applications, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 19 Gbps. Benchmark data shows strong performance in Geekbench multi-core tests, software compilation, and static web serving, alongside average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and multi-core stress tests. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions, it demonstrates strong efficiency in small-to-medium LLM inference. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without local storage requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong performance in database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU with no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86 server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor at 3.2 GHz
384.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
19 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU resources
Strong benchmark performance in database operations, web serving, and software compilation
